hero.title: Eastern Dawning New Partnership
hero.subtitle: Shaping the Next Decade of Climate Action (2026-2035)
hero.description: Accelerating action and innovation of the coming decade

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.subtitle: Partnership and Profit for People and the Planet
mission.description: Shanghai Climate Week 2026 is positioned as a global platform of climate action, innovation, and partnership. A city-named platform, Shanghai Climate Week convenes the best expertise, resources, and leadership in advancing a clean, green, smart, and resilient future from China, Asia, and worldwide. Together, we create and enhance natural capital, human capital, social capital, and financial capital that benefit people and the planet.

registration.question: How do I register for Shanghai Climate Week 2026?
registration.answer: Create an account on the website, then visit the events page to register for the sessions you want to attend. Some events may require approval.

fees.question: Do I need to pay to attend?
fees.answer: Most sessions are free to attend. Some closed-door meetings and special dinners may be paid or invitation-only. Please review each event page for details.

partnership.question: How can my organization become a partner?
partnership.answer: Use this contact page or email partners@shanghaiclimateweek.org.cn and our partnership team will follow up with you.

speaker.question: Can I apply to become a speaker?
speaker.answer: Yes. Experts and practitioners are welcome to apply. Choose the speaker role during registration and submit the requested information.

interpretation.question: Will interpretation be provided?
interpretation.answer: Major sessions provide Chinese-English simultaneous interpretation. Some side events may operate in a single language, so please confirm before attending.

hero.title: About Shanghai Climate Week
hero.description: Shanghai Climate Week is a global climate action platform positioned as the “Eastern Momentum Point,” connecting China and the world in climate governance and shaping climate action for the next decade (2026-2035).

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.tagline: Partnership and Profit for People and the Planet
mission.description: We believe that only through system reshaping, real action, Eastern momentum, and win-win cooperation can climate goals be achieved in the next decade. Shanghai Climate Week is committed to serving as the “Eastern Momentum Point” for global climate governance, connecting China with the world and turning commitments into action.

system.title: System Reshaping
system.description: Move from commitment-driven ambition to results-oriented climate action

action.title: Real Action
action.description: Deliver credible actions and measurable outcomes

momentum.title: Eastern Momentum
momentum.description: Turn China’s green capacity into momentum for global transformation

winwin.title: Shared Prosperity
winwin.description: Strengthen co-creation, connectivity, and mutual benefit for a shared future

aboutPage.timelineSubtitle: The growth path of Shanghai Climate Week

2024.title: Shanghai Climate Week Launches
2024.description: The inaugural Shanghai Climate Week attracted more than 5,000 participants

2025.title: International Expansion
2025.description: Strategic partnerships were established with UNFCCC and the COP presidency

2026.title: A New Voyage of Eastern Momentum
2026.description: The theme evolves to focus on the critical decade from 2026 to 2035

aboutPage.teamSubtitle: A leadership team committed to advancing climate action

viceChair.name: Zhang Ming
viceChair.bio: Former Director-General of the Climate Change Department at the Ministry of Ecology and Environment

cta.title: Join Our Mission
cta.description: Whether you are a company, institution, or individual, you can contribute to climate action

hero.title: Become a Partner
hero.description: Join Shanghai Climate Week 2026 and work with global climate leaders to shape climate action for the next decade. Through strategic partnership, you can strengthen brand influence and unlock new business opportunities.

partnersPage.tiersSubtitle: Choose the right level of partnership and help accelerate climate action

chief.name: Chief Partner
features: Co-hosting opportunities for G20-level programs within the Global Climate Week network
Joint side events with international institutions such as the UN and ADB
Eligibility to co-host flagship programs such as Future Food
Global executive roundtables for a 500+ CEO audience
International PR coverage and top-tier media interviews
36 sqm standard booth with priority placement

partner.name: Presenting Partner
features: Prominent logo placement in key visual assets
Media exposure and mention in press releases

ecosystem.name: Ecosystem Partner
features: Access to the Shanghai Climate Week ecosystem network
Logo placement in key visual assets
sponsors: Institute of Public and Environmental Affairs

partnersPage.otherWaysSubtitle: Beyond sponsorship, we offer multiple forms of partnership

forum.title: Forum Partner
forum.description: Co-host thematic forums, share industry insight, and build thought leadership

knowledge.title: Knowledge Partner
knowledge.description: Co-publish reports and white papers while convening thematic discussions together

community.title: Community Partner
community.description: Mobilize communities and expand public participation in climate action

showcase.title: Showcase Partner
showcase.description: Present innovative products and solutions in the exhibition area with targeted visibility

cta.title: Ready to join us?
cta.description: Contact our partnership team for a detailed proposal and tailored recommendations

hero.title: Newsroom
hero.subtitle: Stay up to date with Shanghai Climate Week announcements, speakers, and event news

1.title: Registration for Shanghai Climate Week 2026 is now open, with global climate leaders heading to Shanghai
1.excerpt: Under the theme Eastern Momentum, New Voyage, this year’s edition is expected to welcome more than 1,000 participants from around the world to discuss the climate action agenda for the next decade...

2.title: Special Envoy Xie Zhenhua confirmed to attend the opening ceremony and deliver a keynote speech
2.excerpt: China’s Special Envoy for Climate Change Xie Zhenhua has confirmed his attendance at the Shanghai Climate Week 2026 opening ceremony, where he will speak about China’s climate policy and global cooperation...

3.title: Partner lineup announced for the Green Finance Innovation Summit
3.excerpt: The People’s Bank of China, the Green Finance Committee, MSCI, Goldman Sachs, and other institutions will jointly host the summit to explore ESG investment and sustainable finance...

4.title: Shanghai Climate Week signs a strategic cooperation agreement with UNDP
4.excerpt: The two sides will deepen collaboration in climate governance, capacity building, and technology transfer to accelerate climate action across the Asia-Pacific region...

5.title: Applications open for the Youth Climate Leaders Summit
5.excerpt: Open to global climate leaders aged 18 to 35, the summit will offer dialogue with leading climate experts and support outstanding projects...

6.title: Tesla and BYD confirmed for the Renewable Energy Transition Conference
6.excerpt: The two EV leaders will showcase their latest electric mobility technologies and sustainable energy solutions to support transport decarbonization...

faqPage.title: FAQ
faqPage.subtitle: Frequently asked questions about Shanghai Climate Week 2026

q1.question: How do I register for Shanghai Climate Week 2026?
q1.answer: You can register through our official website. Click the "Register" button in the top right corner and fill in your basic information to complete registration. After registration, you can sign up for events that interest you.

q2.question: Is there a fee to attend events?
q2.answer: Most events at Shanghai Climate Week 2026 are free and open to the public. Some exclusive closed-door sessions or workshops may require a fee. Please check the event details page for specific information.

q3.question: How can I become a speaker or partner?
q3.answer: If you wish to be a speaker, please select the "Speaker" role during registration, and our team will review your application. Organizations interested in becoming partners can submit an application through the "Partners" page or contact our partnership team directly.

q4.question: Where can I view the event schedule?
q4.answer: You can view the complete schedule on the "Events" page of the website. All events are categorized by date and theme, and you can filter and register according to your interests.

q5.question: Can I cancel my event registration?
q5.answer: Yes, you can cancel registered events in the "My Schedule" section of your personal dashboard. Please cancel at least 24 hours before the event starts so we can release the spot to other participants.

q6.question: Will events offer online participation?
q6.answer: Some important forums and summits will offer live streaming. You can check the event details page to see if live streaming is available. Online participation also requires advance registration.

q7.question: Where are the event venues located?
q7.answer: The main venues for Shanghai Climate Week 2026 include the Shanghai International Convention Center, Shanghai Tower, Zhangjiang Science City, and others. Please check each event's details page for specific addresses.

q8.question: How do I get to the event venues?
q8.answer: All venues are located in easily accessible areas of Shanghai and can be reached by subway, bus, or taxi. Detailed transportation guides will be sent to registered participants before the events.

contact.title: Still have questions?
